differences.title: 1. ORIGIN
differences.leftDesc: Developed and backed by a commercial entity with proprietary elements.
differences.rightDesc: Built and maintained by the open-source community with transparent development.

differences.title: 2. LICENSING
differences.leftDesc: Requires licensing, potentially with usage restrictions and costs.
differences.rightDesc: Freely available, modifiable, and distributable under open-source terms.

differences.title: 3. EXTENSIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Extensibility options might be limited by the commercial roadmap.
differences.rightDesc: Users can contribute, modify, and extend without external constraints.

differences.title: 4. COMMUNITY SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Support primarily through official documentation and company-provided channels.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on community forums, GitHub issues, and collective knowledge sharing.

differences.title: 5. DEVELOPMENT MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Internal development cycles and proprietary code base.
differences.rightDesc: Publicly accessible code, allowing for community contributions and audits.

differences.title: 6. ROADMAP CONTROL
differences.leftDesc: Feature development follows the commercial strategy and priorities.
differences.rightDesc: Direction shaped by community needs, contributions, and popular demand.

differences.title: 7. LONG-TERM VIABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Dependent on the continued success and investment of the owning company.
differences.rightDesc: Less risk of discontinuation if the community remains active and supportive.
